title: Abortion Abolition Bill Introduced in Washington
author: James Silberman
orientation: Reports the March 21, 2019 introduction of WA HB2154, the Abolition of Abortion in Washington Act, by Rep. Matt Shea and three Republican co-sponsors, framing it as the fifth such state bill and arguing that even in deep-blue Washington, the abolitionist demand must be made because incremental pro-life laws teach the wrong lesson and judicial supremacy is constitutionally false.
audiences: Skeptic who thinks abolition bills are pointless in blue states

questions.question: Who introduced the Abolition of Abortion in Washington Act and when?
questions.answer: State Representative Matt Shea (R-Spokane) introduced the bill on March 21st, 2019, with co-sponsors Reps. Vicki Kraft (R-Vancouver), Brad Klippert (R-Kennewick), and Jesse Young (R-Gig Harbor).
questions.quote: On March 21st, State Representative Matt Shea (R-Spokane) introduced a bill that would end abortion in Washington State. The bill is co-sponsored by Reps. Vicki Kraft (R-Vancouver), Brad Klippert (R-Kennewick), and Jesse Young (R-Gig Harbor).

questions.question: How does WA HB2154 define when human life begins, and what legal status does it grant the unborn?
questions.answer: The bill recognizes life as beginning at fertilization and, mirroring the 14th Amendment, requires equal protection of the laws for all human beings including the unborn, which would classify abortion as homicide.
questions.quote: The Abolition of Abortion in Washington Act (WA HB2154) would recognize the observable truth that human physical life begins at “the moment of fertilization upon the fusion of a human spermatozoon with a human ovum.” Mirroring the wording of the 14th amendment of the US Constitution, the bill establishes that all human beings, including the unborn, shall be “[p]rovide[d] the equal protection of the laws of this state.” Abortion would thus be classified as homicide.

questions.question: How does the bill respond to potential federal court interference?
questions.answer: Section 7 declares any federal act, law, treaty, order, regulation, or court decision that prohibits protecting the unborn's right to life null, void, and unenforceable in Washington, and denies federal courts jurisdiction over the state's protection of human life from fertilization.
questions.quote: Any act, law, treaty, order, or regulation of the United States government that denies or prohibits protection of a human person’s inalienable right to life is null, void, and unenforceable in this state. (2) The courts of the United States have no jurisdiction to interfere with this state’s interest in protecting human physical life from the moment that human physical life begins. (3) Any court decision purporting to strike down or enjoin the provisions of this chapter or enjoin this state from protecting innocent human physical life from the moment of fertilization is nonauthoritative, void, and of no force.

questions.question: Where does Washington fit in the national wave of abolition bills, and what's happened to the others?
questions.answer: Washington is the fifth state to introduce such a bill, after Idaho, Indiana, Oklahoma, and Texas; Idaho, Indiana, and Oklahoma efforts were killed by Republican leadership and National Right to Life, while Texas's TX HB896 was still alive and expected to receive a hearing on April 8th.
questions.quote: Washington is the fifth state to introduce such a bill, following [Idaho](https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1975827382724998), [Indiana](https://freethestates.org/2019/03/indiana-pro-life-leaders-kill-protection-at-conception-act/index.html), [Oklahoma](https://freethestates.org/2019/02/the-three-pro-life-men-keeping-abortion-legal-in-oklahoma/index.html), and Texas. The Idaho, Indiana, and Oklahoma efforts have been killed by Republican leadership and National Right to Life. The effort to abolish abortion in Texas is still alive, as TX HB896 is expected to receive a hearing on April 8th.

questions.question: Why pursue an abolition bill in Washington when Democrats control every lever of state government?
questions.audience: Skeptic who thinks abolition bills are pointless in blue states
questions.answer: Silberman argues the law functions as a teacher (citing Galatians 3) and that the legislative pro-life movement has been teaching false exceptions; the message the culture must hear is the unconditional prohibition 'you shall not murder,' so the demand must be made even where passage looks impossible.
questions.quote: Still, it is crucially important that the demand for abortion’s abolition be made. As Paul wrote in Galatians 3, the law is a teacher. And what has the legislative wing of the pro-life movement been teaching the culture? You shall not murder unless the victim is killed with a certain technique? You shall not murder unless the victim is incapable of pain? You shall not murder unless the victim doesn’t yet have a detectable heartbeat? The message the world needs to hear is “you shall not murder.”

questions.question: What does the article claim incremental pro-life laws teach besides accepting some abortions?
questions.answer: They also teach that unconditional submission to the Supreme Court is required, which the author rejects as a fiction contradicted by an honest reading of Article VI of the U.S. Constitution.
questions.quote: Not only that, but incremental laws teach people that unconditional submission to the Supreme Court is [required of them](https://lessermagistrate.com/why-trigger-laws-are-prolonging-the-slaughter-of-the-preborn/). This is not the case. An honest reading of Article VI of the US Constitution clearly contradicts the fiction of judicial supremacy.

questions.question: What historical authority does Silberman cite against judicial supremacy?
questions.answer: He quotes Thomas Jefferson's 1820 letter to Charles Jarvis warning that treating judges as ultimate arbiters of constitutional questions would place the nation under 'the despotism of an oligarchy,' since the Constitution makes all departments co-equal and co-sovereign.
questions.quote: You seem to consider the judges as the ultimate arbiters of all constitutional questions; a very dangerous doctrine indeed, and one which would place us under the despotism of an oligarchy. Our judges are as honest as other men, and not more so. They have, with others, the same passions for party, for power, and the privilege of their corps…. Their power [is] the more dangerous as they are in office for life, and not responsible, as the other functionaries are, to the elective control. The Constitution has erected no such single tribunal, knowing that to whatever hands confided, with the corruptions of time and party, its members would become despots. It has more wisely made all the departments co-equal and co-sovereign within themselves.

questions.question: What scale of killing does the article say abolition would address in Washington?
questions.answer: Roughly 17,000 unborn children are murdered annually in Washington, and the author urges prayer that HB2154 would one day become law.
questions.quote: With roughly 17,000 murdered annually in Washington, pray that it will one day become law.
